stories.title: 'A dangerous proposition': How AI is warping the social fabric and the ways we collectively imagine the future
stories.simple_summary: A news article discusses concerns that artificial intelligence is changing how people think about the future and affecting how society works together.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a general opinion piece about AI's effects on society — worth thinking about, but nothing specific you need to do or worry about today.

stories.title: The apps, gadgets, and tools every reader needs
stories.simple_summary: A magazine published a list of recommended apps, gadgets, and other tools.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly doesn't relate to AI — it's just a product recommendation article you might find interesting if you like tech reviews.

stories.title: Opinion | China’s A.I. Play Is Different From America’s
stories.simple_summary: An article compares how China is approaching artificial intelligence development differently than the United States.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly relevant to investors and government policy — there's nothing specific a small business owner needs to change about how they work.

stories.title: Neil Rimer thinks the AI money is coming back out
stories.simple_summary: An investor predicts that the huge amounts of money being made from AI in tech companies will eventually have to be shared more widely.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and wealthy tech people — nothing affecting your daily business or life right now.

stories.title: Vertu wants executives to pay $6,880 for an AI agent — here’s how it actually performs
stories.simple_summary: A luxury phone company is selling a special AI helper feature on a $6,880 foldable phone, and a tech reviewer checked out how well it actually works.
stories.why_it_matters: This is only relevant if you're interested in expensive luxury phones — it's not a tool for regular business owners.

stories.title: Databricks hits $188B valuation, extending its run as AI’s favorite second act
stories.simple_summary: A company called Databricks reached a very high value in the stock market by focusing on AI tools that help with software coding.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors — nothing you need to act on for your own business.

stories.title: What to know about the AI chip stock selloff - ABC News
stories.source: Breaking News, Latest News and Videos
stories.simple_summary: The prices of stocks for companies that make AI computer chips have been going down recently.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly affects investors and tech companies — it doesn't directly change how small business owners use AI tools.

stories.title: The Zoom hack that says, ‘Don’t record me’
stories.simple_summary: A trick allows people to prevent their Zoom meeting recordings from being automatically transcribed and summarized by AI.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use Zoom meetings and worry about privacy or recordings, this is useful to know — there's a way to stop automatic AI transcripts if you want.

stories.title: Agility Robotics plants its flag in Tesla’s backyard
stories.simple_summary: A robotics company is opening a training center for its robot workers near Tesla's factory in California.
stories.why_it_matters: This matters mainly to large manufacturers — unless you own a factory or warehouse, this doesn't affect you.

stories.title: AI-driven memory crunch jolts India’s smartphone market
stories.simple_summary: In India, people are buying fewer smartphones because AI features are making new phones more expensive.
stories.why_it_matters: This shows how AI is changing product prices worldwide, but for most small business owners, nothing you need to act on today.

stories.title: TikTok is testing an AI likeness detection tool
stories.simple_summary: TikTok is testing a tool that helps creators spot fake AI versions of themselves in videos and report them to the company.
stories.why_it_matters: If you or your business use TikTok, this is helpful protection against someone creating a fake AI copy of you — worth knowing about.

stories.title: MLB restricts using dugout iPads for AI-assisted in-game strategy
stories.simple_summary: Major League Baseball is stopping teams from using AI-powered tablets in the dugout to help decide game strategy during games.
stories.why_it_matters: This is sports news only — it doesn't affect anyone outside of professional baseball.
